2603.10725.title: Towards Robust Speech Deepfake Detection via Human-Inspired Reasoning
2603.10725.paper_summary: The modern generative audio models can be used by an adversary in an unlawful manner, specifically, to impersonate other people to gain access to private information. To mitigate this issue, speech deepfake detection (SDD) methods started to evolve. Unfortunately, current SDD methods generally suffer from the lack of generalization to new audio domains and generators. More than that, they lack interpretability, especially human-like reasoning that would naturally explain the attribution of a given audio to the bona fide or spoof class and provide human-perceptible cues. In this paper, we propose HIR-SDD, a novel SDD framework that combines the strengths of Large Audio Language Models (LALMs) with the chain-of-thought reasoning derived from the novel proposed human-annotated dataset. Experimental evaluation demonstrates both the effectiveness of the proposed method and its ability to provide reasonable justifications for predictions.
2603.10725.paper_summary_zh: 現代生成音頻模型可以被對手以不法的方式使用，具體而言，是模仿其他人以獲取私人信息。為了緩解這個問題，語音深偽檢測（SDD）方法開始發展。不幸的是，當前的SDD方法通常缺乏對新音頻領域和生成器的泛化能力。更重要的是，它們缺乏可解釋性，特別是人類般的推理，這自然會解釋給定音頻屬於真實或偽造類別的原因，並提供人類可感知的線索。在本文中，我們提出HIR-SDD，一個新穎的SDD框架，結合了大型音頻語言模型（LALMs）的優勢與來自新提出的人類標註數據集的思維鏈推理。實驗評估展示了所提方法的有效性及其提供合理預測理由的能力。
2603.10725.author: Artem Dvirniak et.al.
2603.10725.authors: Artem Dvirniak, Evgeny Kushnir, Dmitrii Tarasov, Artem Iudin, Oleg Kiriukhin, Mikhail Pautov, Dmitrii Korzh, Oleg Y. Rogov
